title: [cross-impl] Python sim/node.py dataclass has duplicate seed field
description: node.py:56 and :58 both declare `seed: int = 0`, and __init__ params duplicate it at lines 74 and 77. This is a merge artifact from hnyw/l3j5 CCP-9/TDMA/seed/hop_schedule conflict resolution (see generate.py hash_32 oracle and test_vectors). Prevents importing lichen.sim.*, breaks cross_impl_interop tests, test_gpsr_sim.py, all sim tests. Rust and Zephyr lichen_hash_32 + announce parsing match reference (0x811c9dc5 empty, 0xafd071e5 "test", 0x0b2ae445 zero32; announce rx_channel at wire offset 1, signed_data at byte 42). Python side inconsistent due to this syntax error. Last 20 commits (mostly hnyw.*:fix + bd auto + merges) touched announce.rs, identity.rs, neighbor_monitor.rs, rpl_stack.rs, udp.rs, storage.rs, dispatch.rs, Cargo files -- all Rust alignment work but introduced/failed to resolve Python merge residue. Priority 1 as it blocks vector validation and interop testing.
